site.name: Carlos Manuel Cejas
site.title: Computer Science Student
site.tagline: BSc/MSc Computer Science at Trinity College Dublin. I build software across web, mobile, robotics, and AI; from path planning for autonomous racing to multimodal health apps.
site.workAuth: U.S. Citizen with full work authorization in the United States

education.detail: Relevant: AP Computer Science A, Multi Variable Calculus, Engineering Design

bullets: Develop and refine path-planning algorithms in Python for the autonomous race car subsystem.
Analyze and improve ROS-based codebase; test algorithms in Gazebo simulations.
Optimize racing lines for higher speeds and reduced lap distance.
Led development of company website, building new features and optimizing existing systems.
Consulted on operations, branding, and marketing strategies for early-stage growth.

featuredProjects.name: Sol-450
featuredProjects.description: Full-stack, multi-agent greenhouse simulator for long-duration Mars missions: configure a colony, run AI-managed agriculture, and track crew survival. React 19 and Three.js for 3D colony visualization; FastAPI simulation engine; Strands agents on Amazon Bedrock with an MCP knowledge base; DynamoDB session state.
featuredProjects.tech: React, Three.js, FastAPI, AWS Bedrock, DynamoDB, JavaScript, Python

featuredProjects.name: Blackout
featuredProjects.description: Group phone-free sessions with real app blocking: a host shares a link or QR, sets the end time and optional breaks, and joiners stay locked until the session ends-with in-session chat, host announcements, and push when you need updates without opening other apps. One product on iOS and Android. I cofounded Blackout and work across mobile, backend, and web.
featuredProjects.tech: Native iOS and Android (Swift, Kotlin) with focus/app-limit APIs, plus cloud backend, real-time chat, APNs/FCM notifications, and invite links.

featuredProjects.name: AI Dungeon Master
featuredProjects.context: Claude Builder Club @ TCD Hackathon - Dec 2025
featuredProjects.description: Hackathon winner (Best Team Collaboration, Most Creative Use of Claude). Web-based D&D with AI-driven storytelling, persistent campaigns, and randomized characters. Originally Claude-powered; codebase now uses Google Gemini.
featuredProjects.tech: Python, FastAPI, React, Vite, Gemini API

featuredProjects.name: Pollen Cast
featuredProjects.description: Multimodal allergy health-check app (Expo/React Native): eye photo, optional voice, and location for assessment. Node backend with Gemini and optional Google Pollen API; Supabase auth; optional Python (librosa) voice service; safety guardrails and HL7 FHIR-oriented outputs.
featuredProjects.tech: React Native (Expo), Node.js, Express, Gemini API, Supabase, Python (librosa)

featuredProjects.name: Metricare
featuredProjects.description: Medical patient dashboard unifying history, medications, FDA-based drug interaction checks, and AI-generated summaries (Gemini) - one Metricare UI and API instead of scattered legacy tools.
featuredProjects.tech: React, Vite, FastAPI, TypeScript, Python, Gemini API

skills.languages: Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, Java, HTML/CSS, SQL, Bash
skills.frameworks: React, React Native (Expo), Vite, FastAPI, Node.js, Express, Three.js, ROS, librosa
skills.tools: Git, Docker, Linux (Ubuntu/Arch), AWS (Bedrock, DynamoDB), Supabase, Vercel, Gazebo, VS Code
skills.ai: Gemini API, Claude API, agent patterns (Strands, MCP / knowledge bases), Google Pollen API, HL7 FHIR R4, SNOMED-CT, OpenFDA (drug search & interactions)
